metadata_information.title: Indoor air quality for poor families: new evidence from Bangladesh
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Indoor air quality for poor families: new evidence from Bangladesh

document_description.abstract: Indoor air pollution (IAP) from cooking and heating is estimated to kill a million children annually in developing countries. To promote a better understanding of IAP, the authors investigate the determinants of IAP in Bangladesh using the latest air monitoring technology and a national household survey. The study concludes that IAP is dangerously high for many poor families in Bangladesh. Concentrations of respirable airborne particulates(PM10) 300 ug/
